name: Safra National Bank Of New York
description_short: Safra National Bank is a private bank established in 1987 specializing in wealth management and customized banking solutions for ultra-high-net-worth clients.
description_long:
Safra National Bank is a privately-owned institution established in 1987, operating as part of the broader J. Safra Group—a family-owned conglomerate with over 180 years of banking heritage tracing back to Aleppo, Syria in the mid-nineteenth century. The bank is FDIC-insured and positioned as a leading global private bank with presence in more than 25 countries across 200+ locations. The organization emphasizes long-term stability, family ownership, and conservative risk management as core pillars of its business model.

The bank offers comprehensive private banking services tailored exclusively to ultra-high-net-worth individuals, families, and corporate clients. Service offerings include sophisticated portfolio solutions, comprehensive banking services, bespoke lending solutions, corporate banking support, and dedicated services for external managers and family offices. The bank markets itself as providing personal financial expertise with fast decision-making capabilities for high-complexity wealth management needs.

Safra National Bank distinguishes itself through several factors: 180+ years of family banking heritage and global network leverage; private family ownership ensuring long-term consistency and stability; a stated "robust CET1 ratio" supporting financial strength; a conservative, time-tested approach to risk management; and personalized, relationship-driven client service model. The bank explicitly positions itself as a reliable long-term banking partner with emphasis on discretion, security, and multigenerational wealth preservation.

As an exclusive private bank, Safra National Bank operates with substantial minimum account and service requirements not disclosed on public-facing materials. The institution does not provide retail consumer banking products, meaning accessibility is limited to ultra-high-net-worth clients. While the website emphasizes stability and strength, specific details regarding fees, minimum deposits, interest rates, and service availability remain unavailable to prospective retail clients. The bank's focus on relationship banking means applications and onboarding processes differ significantly from conventional banking institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Private banking and wealth management for ultra-high-net-worth individuals and families
Sophisticated portfolio solutions and customized financial strategies
Bespoke lending solutions tailored to complex client needs
Corporate banking and business financing services
Family office banking and dedicated services for external asset managers
Comprehensive banking services for multi-generational wealth structures
International banking and cross-border transaction facilitation
Personalized financial advisory and expert guidance services
Investment products and portfolio management oversight
Fast decision-making on complex banking requests
Long-term relationship banking with dedicated relationship managers
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government full faith and credit
Over 180 years of family banking heritage and proven institutional continuity
Global presence in 200+ locations across 25+ countries enabling international wealth management
Private family ownership structure ensures long-term stability without pressure for quarterly earnings maximization
Stated robust CET1 capital ratio indicating strong financial reserve position
Personalized relationship-based service model with dedicated experts for complex wealth structures
Conservative risk management philosophy tested across multiple economic cycles
cons: Exclusive ultra-high-net-worth focus means prohibitively high minimum account requirements making access impossible for typical consumers
Limited public transparency on fees, rates, minimum deposits, and specific service terms
No retail consumer banking products available; relationship banking model requires direct contact with private client teams
Website content lacks specific information on account types, APYs, lending rates, or comparable competitive metrics
Not suitable for clients seeking transparent, self-service, or digital-first banking experiences
best_for: Ultra-high-net-worth individuals ($50M+ in assets) requiring sophisticated multi-jurisdictional wealth management
Family offices and external asset managers needing private banking infrastructure and liquidity solutions
Established businesses and corporate entities requiring relationship-based banking partnerships with substantial capital access
diagnosis: Safra National Bank is exclusively designed for ultra-high-net-worth individuals, families, and institutions requiring sophisticated private banking services and global wealth management infrastructure. The primary caveat is that this bank operates exclusively in the private banking segment with undisclosed minimum requirements, making it inaccessible to typical consumers seeking retail banking products, and requiring substantial assets to qualify for account opening.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
fdic_official_name: Safra National Bank of New York
